issue_title: Completed tasks restore on shutdown causing duplicates for recursive tasks
issue_body:
# Checklist * [x] I have read the relevant section in the [contribution guide](http://docs.celeryproject.org/en/latest/contributing.html#other-bugs) on reporting bugs. * [x] I have checked the [issues list](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Issue+Type%3A+Bug+Report%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for similar or identical bug reports. * [x] I have checked the [pull requests list](https://github.com/celery/celery/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22PR+Type%3A+Bugfix%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for existing proposed fixes. * [x] I have checked the [commit log](https://github.com/celery/celery/commits/master) to find out if the bug was already fixed in the master branch. * [x] I have included all related issues and possible duplicate issues in this issue (If there are none, check this box anyway). ## Mandatory Debugging Information * [x] I have included the output of ``celery -A proj report`` in the issue. (if you are not able to do this, then at least specify the Celery version affected). * [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `master` branch of Celery. * [x] I have included the contents of ``pip freeze`` in the issue. * [x] I have included all the versions of all the external dependencies required to reproduce this bug. ## Optional Debugging Information * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one Python version and/or implementation. * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one message broker and/or result backend. * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one version of the message broker and/or result backend. * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one operating system. * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one workers pool. * [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue with autoscaling, retries, ETA/Countdown & rate limits disabled. * [x] I have tried reproducing the issue after downgrading and/or upgrading Celery and its dependencies. ## Related Issues and Possible Duplicates #### Related Issues * None #### Possible Duplicates * None ## Environment & Settings **4.3.0 (rhubarb)**:
celery report Output:

``` $ celery -A tasks report software -> celery:4.3.0 (rhubarb) kombu:4.6.3 py:2.7.15rc1 billiard:3.6.0.0 redis:3.2.1 platform -> system:Linux arch:64bit kernel version:4.15.0-55-generic imp:CPython loader -> celery.loaders.app.AppLoader settings -> transport:redis results:disabled broker_url: u'redis://localhost:6379/0' ```

# Steps to Reproduce ## Required Dependencies * **Minimal Python Version**: 2.7 * **Minimal Celery Version**: 4.0 * **Minimal Kombu Version**: N/A or Unknown * **Minimal Broker Version**: N/A or Unknown * **Minimal Result Backend Version**: N/A or Unknown * **Minimal OS and/or Kernel Version**: N/A or Unknown * **Minimal Broker Client Version**: N/A or Unknown * **Minimal Result Backend Client Version**: N/A or Unknown ### Python Packages
pip freeze Output:

``` amqp==2.5.0 billiard==3.6.0.0 celery==4.3.0 gevent==1.3.7 greenlet==0.4.15 kombu==4.6.3 pytz==2019.1 redis==3.2.1 vine==1.3.0 ```

### Other Dependencies

N/A

## Minimally Reproducible Test Case

```python import time from celery import Celery app = Celery('tasks', broker='redis://localhost:6379/0') @app.task def recurse(num_times, sleep_seconds=1): print("Recursing... num_times={}".format(num_times)) if sleep_seconds > 0: time.sleep(sleep_seconds) if num_times > 0: recurse.delay(num_times-1) print("Recursed... num_times={}".format(num_times)) ```

# Expected Behavior When I stop the worker all tasks will finish, acknowledging that they've finished. # Actual Behavior Celery will pick up more work after warm shutdown. That work will complete, in the case of recursive tasks will queue the next task, and then be restored because the task was never acked. This seems to be because the synloop shuts down without flushing pending operations and the acks are handled here. ``` [celery]gary@gir:~/celery-test$ celery worker -l debug -A tasks -P gevent -c 10 -Q celery -n worker-2 --without-gossip --without-mingle [2019-07-31 20:14:28,058: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Worker: Preparing bootsteps. [2019-07-31 20:14:28,059: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Worker: Building graph... [2019-07-31 20:14:28,059: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Worker: New boot order: {StateDB, Timer, Hub, Pool, Autoscaler, Beat, Consumer} [2019-07-31 20:14:28,065: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Preparing bootsteps. [2019-07-31 20:14:28,065: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Building graph... [2019-07-31 20:14:28,067: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: New boot order: {Connection, Events, Mingle, Tasks, Control, Heart, Gossip, Agent, event loop} -------------- celery@worker-2 v4.3.0 (rhubarb) ---- **** ----- --- * *** * -- Linux-4.15.0-55-generic-x86_64-with-Ubuntu-18.04-bionic 2019-07-31 20:14:28 -- * - **** --- - ** ---------- [config] - ** ---------- .> app: tasks:0x7f3c790fe4d0 - ** ---------- .> transport: redis://localhost:6379/0 - ** ---------- .> results: disabled:// - *** --- * --- .> concurrency: 10 (gevent) -- ******* ---- .> task events: OFF (enable -E to monitor tasks in this worker) --- ***** ----- -------------- [queues] .> celery exchange=celery(direct) key=celery [tasks] . celery.accumulate . celery.backend_cleanup . celery.chain . celery.chord . celery.chord_unlock . celery.chunks . celery.group . celery.map . celery.starmap . tasks.recurse [2019-07-31 20:14:28,071: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Worker: Starting Pool [2019-07-31 20:14:28,071: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,071: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Worker: Starting Consumer [2019-07-31 20:14:28,071: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting Connection [2019-07-31 20:14:28,077: INFO/MainProcess] Connected to redis://localhost:6379/0 [2019-07-31 20:14:28,077: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,077: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting Events [2019-07-31 20:14:28,080: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,080: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting Tasks [2019-07-31 20:14:28,082: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,082: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting Control [2019-07-31 20:14:28,082: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,082: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting Heart [2019-07-31 20:14:28,086: DEBUG/MainProcess] ^-- substep ok [2019-07-31 20:14:28,086: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Starting event loop [2019-07-31 20:14:28,087: INFO/MainProcess] celery@worker-2 ready. [2019-07-31 20:14:45,466: DEBUG/MainProcess] Task accepted: tasks.recurse[1eaf15d1-f0c1-4a65-bd6f-f4b593c28a1d] pid:3828 [2019-07-31 20:14:45,466: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=6 [2019-07-31 20:14:45,466: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Stopping Control... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,471: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=6 [2019-07-31 20:14:46,471: INFO/MainProcess] Task tasks.recurse[1eaf15d1-f0c1-4a65-bd6f-f4b593c28a1d] succeeded in 1.00525897399s: None [2019-07-31 20:14:46,472: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Shutdown Heart... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,472: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Shutdown Control... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,472: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Shutdown Tasks... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,473: DEBUG/MainProcess] Canceling task consumer... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,473: DEBUG/MainProcess] Closing consumer channel... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,473: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Shutdown Events... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,474: DEBUG/MainProcess] | Consumer: Shutdown Connection... [2019-07-31 20:14:46,475: WARNING/MainProcess] Restoring 1 unacknowledged message(s) ``` You can see if you run the worker again (sans debug this time for brevity) it starts running two tasks ``` [celery]gary@gir:~/celery-test$ celery worker -A tasks -P gevent -c 10 -Q celery -n worker-2 --without-gossip --without-mingle -------------- celery@worker-2 v4.3.0 (rhubarb) ---- **** ----- --- * *** * -- Linux-4.15.0-55-generic-x86_64-with-Ubuntu-18.04-bionic 2019-07-31 20:34:06 -- * - **** --- - ** ---------- [config] - ** ---------- .> app: tasks:0x7f7b1ccad4d0 - ** ---------- .> transport: redis://localhost:6379/0 - ** ---------- .> results: disabled:// - *** --- * --- .> concurrency: 10 (gevent) -- ******* ---- .> task events: OFF (enable -E to monitor tasks in this worker) --- ***** ----- -------------- [queues] .> celery exchange=celery(direct) key=celery [2019-07-31 20:34:06,031: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=6 [2019-07-31 20:34:06,033: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=5 [2019-07-31 20:34:07,052: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=5 [2019-07-31 20:34:07,052: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=6 [2019-07-31 20:34:07,053: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=5 [2019-07-31 20:34:07,054: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=4 ^C worker: Hitting Ctrl+C again will terminate all running tasks! worker: Warm shutdown (MainProcess) [2019-07-31 20:34:08,059: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=5 [2019-07-31 20:34:08,060: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=4 [2019-07-31 20:34:08,061: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursing... num_times=4 [2019-07-31 20:34:09,065: WARNING/MainProcess] Recursed... num_times=4 [2019-07-31 20:34:09,067: WARNING/MainProcess] Restoring 1 unacknowledged message(s) ``` This behavior is new in 4.x. With the same code I'm unable to replicate in 3.1.26. This behavior is very reproducible in 4.x and causes very bad duplication when workers exist in autoscaling environments. I believe this regression was introduced in 7ec89a6bf0da853fc9f7e3e9911faf86880178d6 The following patch fixes this behavior but I'm not sure it is desirable as is and would like some guidance. ``` $ git diff diff --git a/celery/worker/consumer/consumer.py b/celery/worker/consumer/consumer.py index 60e64c298..81aa96b1a 100644 --- a/celery/worker/consumer/consumer.py +++ b/celery/worker/consumer/consumer.py @@ -563,8 +563,8 @@ class Consumer(object): try: strategy( message, payload, - promise(call_soon, (message.ack_log_error,)), - promise(call_soon, (message.reject_log_error,)), + message.ack_log_error, + message.reject_log_error, callbacks, ) except (InvalidTaskError, ContentDisallowed) as exc: ```
